rulings.Case Title: WYNSHIP HILLIER VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Deposition Subpoena And Request For Monetary And Issue Sanctions (Cal Code Civ Proc. 2025.480(A) , 2023.040)
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015, Line 1, PETITIONER WYNSHIP HILLIER's Motion To Compel Responses To Deposition Subpoena And Request For Monetary And Issue Sanctions (Cal Code Civ Proc. 2025.480(A) , 2023.040). Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Petitioner WYNSHIP HILLIER's Motion to Compel is DENIED. The Custodian of Records for Deponent has stated under oath on 2/5/15 that it does not have the documents requested in the deposition subpoena served on 1/23/15. The request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LAW OFFICES OF IAN ZIMMERMAN VS. MIRTHA ESTRADA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion (Petition) And Motion (Petition) Regarding Petition To Vacate/Correct Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT MIRTHA ESTRADA, ASHWIN LADVA, ESQ. Motion (Petition) Regarding Petition To Vacate/Correct Arbitration Award. Denied. First, the parties explicitly stipulated on the record at the arbitration that they were waiving their right to appeal the arbitration award, with the sole exception for allegations of fraud. (Declaration of Eric Tosaris, Ex. F, Arbitration Hearing Transcript, p. 11:10 - 12:3.) There are no allegations of fraud here. The waiver is valid and enforceable. (See Pratt v. Gursey, Schneider & Co. (2000) 80 Cal.App.4th 1105, 1107.) Second, even if the parties did not waive the right to appeal, Petitioners have not established that the arbitrator exceeded his powers. Petitioners explicitly agreed that the arbitrator would have the authority to "cover and resolve any and all disputes as to fees and/or costs between all of the attorneys and Mirtha Estrada." (Declaration of Eric Tosaris, Ex. E.) The arbitration award does precisely that. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/ehg)

rulings.Case Title: KELLY O'HAIRE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities (Redacted)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, GREG SUHR MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities (Redacted). The motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. There are triable issues of material regarding whether plaintiff can establish the retaliation cause of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff's disclosure can support the claim. See Hager v. County of Los Angeles (2014) 228 Cal.App.4th 1538. The court finds Hager to be better reasoned than Mize-Kurzman v. Marin Community College Dist. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 832, and relies on Hager. See McCallum v. McCallum (1987) 190 Cal.App.3d 308, 315, fn. 4. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff reasonably believed that Chief Suhr was violating the Penal Code. There are triable issues of material fact regarding whether there is a causal link between the protected conduct and the adverse employment action and whether the reasons for the termination were pretextual. The trier of fact could find that the timing of the termination decision was suspicious. Chief Suhr's counsel threatened plaintiff (Plaintiff's Dec., par. 10-11). Chief Suhr admitted knowledge of the investigation and plaintiff's role. (Suhr depo., 6:10-7:8; 8:6-9:6). Lastly, the declaration of Mr. Tidwell undermines Chief Suhr's proffered reasons for the adverse employment action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff properly filed a government claim with defendant and defendant received it. First, plaintiff declares that she mailed the claim (Plaintiff's Dec., par. 26). Gov't Code sec. 915.2 provides that presentation is made at that time. Second, Sgt. Goss declares that it was Police Legal's practice to forward the claims to the City Attorney's Office, which is in the same building as the Controller. (Goss Dec., par. 9). There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff can establish the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. The court construes the claim as a statutory claim per Gov't 815.2(a) [public employer liable for acts or omissions of its employees] and 820(a) [public employee liable to same extent as private person]. Worker's compensation does not bar the claim because retaliation that violates California's public policy is outside the employment bargain. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/ehg)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK VS. ANTONIO MA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015, Line 9, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Adjudication. Motion for summary adjudication as to the account stated cause of action is granted. Plaintiff met its initial burden by submitting evidence proving each element of the cause of action. (Plaintiff's Undisputed Material Facts, 1-18.) Defendant does not raise a triable issue of material fact. Defendant's objections to the Declaration of Richard Kier are overruled. The Declaration is admissible pursuant to the business records exception from the hearsay rule. (See Cal. Ev. Code sec. 1271.) The Declaration lays adequate foundation and establishes that Mr. Kier has personal knowledge. Plaintiff has standing to maintain this cause of action. (See Cal. Civ. Code sec. 171; 12 U.S.C. sec. 24.) This cause of action is not barred by the statute of limitations under either California or Utah law. (See CCP sec. 337; Utah Code sec. 76B-2-307.) Utah Code sec. 70C-7-205 is inapplicable to this action. The one year statute of limitations provided for in that section applies to actions brought by debtors against credit card companies. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/ehg)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK VS. EZRA STANLEY AKA EZRA J STANLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015, Line 14, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Adjudication. Motion for summary judgment granted. Plaintiff met its initial burden by submitting evidence proving each element of the open book account and account stated causes of action. (Plaintiff's Undisputed Material Facts, 1-18.) Defendant does not raise a triable issue of material fact. First, Defendant's separate statement in opposition does not comply with CCP sec. 437c(b)(3), which requires that each disputed fact "shall be followed by a reference to the supporting evidence." Second, Defendant's declaration, the only evidence submitted in opposition, cannot be considered, as it is not executed under penalty of perjury. (See Kulshrestha v. First Union Comm. Corp. (2004) 33 Cal.4th 601, 608.) Defendant's objections to the Declaration of Linda Salas are overruled. The Declaration establishes that Ms. Salas is a custodian of records. (Salas Declaration, pars. 1-2.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/ehg)

rulings.Case Title: TREVOR GARDNER VS. SAN FRANCISCO LESBIAN, GAY, BISEXUAL, TRANSGENDER et al

rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF TREVOR GARDNER's Motion To Compel Defendant LGBT Pride Celebration Committee, Inc.'s Further Response To Form Interrogatory 15.1 And Special Interrogatories 6-10. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff TREVOR GARDENER's Motion to Compel Further Response to Form Interrogatory 15.1 and Special Interrogatories 6-10 is DENIED. The Court finds that defendant PRIDE's responses to the discovery at issue is sufficient under the terms of agreement between the Parties and the Discovery Act. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL VS. HENRY KARNILOWICZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Henry Karnilowicz Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015, Line 15, Defendant Henry Karnilowicz Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint. Overruled as to the first cause of action: Non-compliance with orders of abatement issued by the San Francisco Department of Public Health. The First Amended Complaint (FAC) is not uncertain, ambiguous or unintelligible. The FAC sufficiently alleges Defendant is targeted by each cause of action (See FAC at 8-9.) The FAC adequately sets forth the ultimate facts necessary to establish each element of the cause of action. (See FAC at 20.) Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the second cause of action: Public Nuisance. The FAC does not allege ultimate facts that Defendant violated the San Francisco Electrical Code. (See FAC at 64.) The Court finds there is a split of authority as to the issue of ultimate facts necessary to survive demurrer. (See City of Bakersfield v. Miller, 64 Cal. 2d 93, 97, 410 P.2d 393, 396 (1966); People v. McKale, 25 Cal. 3d 626, 631, 602 P.2d 731, 733 (1979); Sheehan v. San Francisco 49ers, Ltd., 45 Cal. 4th 992, 997, 201 P.3d 472, 476 (2009). Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the third cause of action: Unlawful, unfair, and fraudulent business practices. Plaintiff must state with reasonable particularity the facts supporting the statutory elements of the violation. Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc., 14 Cal. App. 4th 612, 615, 17 Cal. Rptr. 2d 708, 710 (1993). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/ehg)

rulings.Case Title: NETFORTIRIS ACQUISITON CO INC SUCCESSOR TO VS. NATIONAL MERCHANT CENTER , INC ET AL** TRANSFERRED TO ORANGE COUNTY **
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015, Line 12, DEFENDANT NATIONAL MERCHANT CENTER , INC. Motion To Transfer Action. Granted. This case is transferred to Orange County. Pursuant to CCP sec. 395.5, venue is proper in the county where the corporation's principal place of business is located, where the contract is made or is to be performed, or where the breach occurs. It is undisputed that Defendant's principal place of business is Orange County. Paragraph 4 of the Declaration of Roman Balanko establishes that the contract was entered into in Orange County because that is where "the defendant's obligation was incurred." (Braunstein v. Sup. Ct. (1964) 225 Cal.App.2d 691, 694.) The contract provides that the services were to be provided in Orange County. (Complaint, Ex. B, Telekenex Information Services Supplement, par. 5.) The alleged breach occurred in Los Angeles County, where Plaintiff admits payment was due. (Declaration of Marilou King, par. 8.) Thus, there is no basis for venue in San Francisco County.
